PlatformIntegrations

Integrations

Connect ClawOps to the tools your business already runs. Each integration is set up once and unlocked across all AI agents — no code required.

n8n Workflow Automation

Popular

Connect your AI agents to 400+ apps via n8n's visual workflow editor. Automate complex sequences, run tasks on schedules, and let AI agents control workflows end-to-end.

Setup Guide
  1. Open n8n — click Open N8n in the Integrations page. It opens at app.clawops.studio/{userId}/n8n
  2. No login needed — you're already authenticated via your ClawOps session. Jump straight into building.
  3. Build your first workflow — use the visual drag-and-drop editor. Start with a simple two-node flow and expand from there.
  4. Add triggers — configure a starting node: choose Webhook (for API calls), Schedule (recurring time-based), or AI Agent (triggered by your ClawOps worker).
  5. Connect to AI agents — copy the Webhook URL from your n8n node and paste it into the AI agent task configuration. The agent calls this URL to trigger the workflow.
  6. Run manually or auto — click Test Workflow to run it right now, or leave it running in the background. It executes on every trigger event.
  7. AI agents trigger workflows — your ClawOps worker can POST data to the webhook, passing context (contact info, social post content, task results) into your n8n flow automatically.
n8n ships with 400+ pre-built app integrations — Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Sheets, Slack, and more.
Use the AI Agent node to let Claude or OpenAI make decisions within your workflow.
Workflows run on schedules even when you're offline. Set it and forget it.
n8n supports error workflows — route failures to a notification channel automatically.
If your workflow accesses external APIs (e.g. Notion, Google), make sure those credentials are stored in n8n Credentials, not hardcoded.
For production workflows, use a persistent activation (turned on by default) rather than manual triggers.

GoHighLevel CRM

CRM

Sync your GHL account to let AI agents manage contacts, deals, pipeline stages, tasks, and email campaigns — directly from the ClawOps dashboard.

Setup Guide
  1. Log into your GoHighLevel account at app.gohighlevel.com
  2. Navigate to Settings → API → API Keys
  3. Copy your Location ID from Settings → Business Info (each location has its own ID)
  4. Click Create New API Key — name it "ClawOps" and grant Read/Write permissions
  5. Copy the generated API Key — you'll paste it into the ClawOps dashboard next
  1. Go to Integrations → GHL in the sidebar
  2. Paste your Location ID into the Location ID field
  3. Paste your API Key into the API Key field
  4. Click Save & Test Connection
  5. A success banner confirms your GHL account is live and authenticated
Each GHL location has a unique Location ID — if you manage multiple locations, connect each one separately.
Start with sandbox/test mode credentials before connecting your production GHL account.
The AI agent can run bulk operations — import 100 contacts from a CSV, update 50 deal statuses in one go.
GHL's Tags and Custom Fields are fully accessible — reference them in natural language prompts.
Your API Key must have Read/Write permissions. Keys with read-only access cannot create contacts or update deals.
Always test in sandbox mode first — API writes to your live CRM are permanent unless undone manually.
GHL rate limits apply: avoid triggering more than 60 API calls per minute in automations.

Composio — Social Media Automation

Free Tier

Connect Twitter/X, LinkedIn, and Instagram via Composio. Your AI agent gains the ability to post, reply, schedule, and track engagement — all from simple chat commands.

Setup Guide
  1. Visit composio.dev and sign up for free using your Gmail account
  2. In the Composio dashboard, click Connect App
  3. Search for Twitter/X — click Authorize and complete the OAuth flow
  4. Search for LinkedIn — click Authorize and grant page/profile permissions
  5. Search for Instagram — click Authorize (Instagram requires a Business or Creator account)
  6. Go to Settings → API Keys → copy your Composio API key
  1. Back in the ClawOps dashboard, go to Social → Composio Settings
  2. Paste your Composio API Key into the API Key field
  3. Click Connect — the status indicator turns green on success
  4. Select which social accounts you want the AI agent to have access to
  5. Click Save — your agent is now authorized to post on your behalf
Composio has a generous free tier — up to 100 API calls/month free, enough for moderate posting.
AI-generated posts are drafted by the agent first. You can set an approval mode to review before publishing.
Connect all three platforms to run cross-posting campaigns with platform-specific formatting.
Composio also supports YouTube, Facebook, Reddit, and Google Business Profile.
Instagram integration requires an Business or Creator account — personal accounts are not supported by Composio.
AI posting with auto-reply is powerful but monitor it closely in the first week to ensure tone and content are correct.
Composio API keys are scoped to your workspace — rotate them from composio.dev if you suspect exposure.
Channels & API Keys

API Keys

Connect any external API — OpenAI, Gemini, Twitter, LinkedIn, ElevenLabs, and more.

  1. Get your API key from the provider's developer portal (OpenAI, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, etc.)
  2. Go to Settings → API Keys in the ClawOps dashboard
  3. Paste the key and give it a label (e.g., "Production – OpenAI")
  4. Click Save — the agent can now call that API directly via plain English prompts
API keys are encrypted at rest and never exposed in logs. Rotate keys from the provider's portal and update them in ClawOps Settings.

Telegram Bot

Fastest setup. Create a bot, paste the token, done — your agent is live 24/7.

  1. Search @BotFather in Telegram → send /newbot → copy the bot token (e.g. 123456789:ABCdef...)
  2. Add to OpenClaw: openclaw channels add telegram --token YOUR_BOT_TOKEN
  3. Search your bot in Telegram → send /start → your AI agent responds instantly
Telegram bots cannot initiate conversations — users must message them first.

WhatsApp Business

Connect WhatsApp Business API for reliable, branded customer messaging.

  1. Option A (recommended): Sign up at business.whatsapp.com → get Phone Number ID + Access Token from Meta Developer Console
  2. Add to OpenClaw: openclaw channels add whatsapp --token ACCESS_TOKEN --phone-id PHONE_ID
  3. Option B: WhatsApp Web via Camoufox — unofficial but fast to set up for personal/business use
WhatsApp Business API requires Meta business verification (1-3 business days). Plan ahead for production deployments.

Discord Bot

Add your AI agent as a Discord bot — responds to slash commands and channel mentions.

  1. Go to discord.com/developers/applications → create app → Bot → copy the bot token
  2. OAuth2 URL Generator → select scopes: bot + applications.commands → permissions: Send Messages + Read Message History → authorize in your server
  3. Add to OpenClaw: openclaw channels add discord --token BOT_TOKEN --guild GUILD_ID

n8n MCP Server

Expose n8n workflows as MCP tools — the AI agent calls them like native functions.

  1. Option A — MCP Server (recommended): npm install -g @n8n/mcp then openclaw mcp add n8n --command n8n-mcp --args "--url https://app.clawops.studio/n8n"
  2. Option B — Webhooks: Add a Webhook trigger node in your n8n workflow → copy the URL → paste into ClawOps agent config
  3. Restart gateway: openclaw gateway restart → agent can now trigger any workflow via plain English
See the full step-by-step guide at Channels & API Keys documentation page for detailed n8n MCP setup with screenshots.

GHL MCP Skill

The ghl-crm skill is pre-installed. Set your API key to unlock full CRM automation.

  1. Get credentials: GHL → Settings → API → create a Private Integration API key. Copy your Location ID from Settings → Business Info
  2. Set environment variables: export GHL_API_KEY="your-private-integration-token" and export GHL_LOCATION_ID="your-location-id"
  3. Use from the agent: "Search for contact john@example.com in GHL" → "Move the Acme deal to Won in pipeline Stage 3" → "Send an SMS to +15551234567"
GHL API keys must have Read/Write permissions. Read-only keys cannot create contacts or update deals. Test in sandbox mode first.
Integration Comparison
Featuren8nGoHighLevelComposio
Workflow builder✅ Visual drag & drop❌ Native only❌ External
CRM contact sync✅ Via integrations✅ Native❌ No
Social posting✅ Via integrations✅ Built-in✅ Native
AI agent trigger✅ Webhook + AI Agent node✅ API + agent commands✅ Agent commands
Schedule automation✅ Cron/schedule nodes✅ Workflow triggers✅ Via agent scheduling
Offline execution✅ Runs 24/7✅ Cloud-only✅ Cloud-only
CostFree (self-hosted) / $20/mo cloud$39/mo starterFree tier available
FAQ
Can I use multiple integrations at the same time?
Absolutely. There's no conflict between n8n, GoHighLevel, and Composio — they complement each other. For example: Composio handles social posting, GHL manages the CRM, and n8n orchestrates the data flow between them.
Do I need paid accounts for these tools?
n8n has a free self-hosted option (you're already running it via ClawOps) and a free cloud tier with 100 executions. GHL requires a paid account ($39/mo starter). Composio has a free tier with 100 API calls/month.
How do I disconnect an integration?
Go to Integrations → [Service Name] → click Disconnect. This revokes API access immediately. Your data in the connected service (contacts in GHL, workflows in n8n) is unaffected.
Can I build custom integrations?
Yes. Use the n8n HTTP Request node to call any REST API. If you need a native ClawOps integration built, contact the team via the dashboard.
Where can I find my n8n webhook URL?
In n8n, open any workflow → click the starting node → copy the Webhook URL shown in the node panel. Paste this URL in the AI agent task configuration under Webhook Trigger.
